name: lying elbow to knee
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engage your abs and lift your upper body off the ground, bringing your right elbow towards your left knee.
At the same time, extend your right leg straight out and lift it off the ground.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, this time bringing your left elbow towards your right knee and extending your left leg.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground. Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards. Engage your abs and lift your upper body off the ground, bringing your right elbow towards your left knee. At the same time, extend your right leg straight out and lift it off the ground. Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position. Repeat the movement, this time bringing your left elbow towards your right knee and extending your left leg. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Avoid supine positions after the first trimester due to vena cava compression. Incline or side-lie alternatives preferred.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
